\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"skipped\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nclass RiskLevel(str, Enum):\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    SAFE = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"safe\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    MODERATE = queue.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nimport json\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nimport uuid\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom typing import Iterable, Optional\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom harness.db import HarnessDB\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nfrom harness.models import (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    PermissionRequestRecord,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n TaskStatus,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndef utcnow_iso() -> str:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nclass TaskQueue:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"CRUD layer for tasks, stages, and audit tables.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def __init__(self, db: HarnessDB) -> None:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        self.db = db\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def create_task(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        self,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        title: str,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        description: str,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        *,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        goal: Optional[str] = None,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        verify_cmd: Optional[str] = None,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        complexity: str = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"medium\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        priority: int = 50,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.PENDING,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        working_dir: Optional[str] = None,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        task_id: Optional[str] = None,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    ) -> TaskRecord:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        record = TaskRecord(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            task_id=task_id or uuid.uuid4().hex,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            title=title,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            description=description,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            goal=goal,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            verify_cmd=verify_cmd,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            complexity=complexity,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            priority=priority,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            status=status,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            working_dir=working_dir,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            created_at=now,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            updated_at=now,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        )\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                INSERT INTO tasks (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    task_id, title, description, goal, verify_cmd, complexity,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    priority, status, working_dir, created_at, updated_at\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.task_id,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.title,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.description,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.goal,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.verify_cmd,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.complexity,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.priority,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.status.value,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.working_dir,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.created_at,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    record.updated_at,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                ),\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            )\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        return record\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def list_tasks(self, status: Optional[TaskStatus] = None) -> list[TaskRecord]:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        query = \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM tasks\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        params: tuple[object, ...] = ()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        if status is not None:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            query += \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" WHERE status = ?\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            params = (status.value,)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        query += \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        return [self._row_to_task(row) for row in rows]\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def pick_next_runnable_task(self) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n stage_id=f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"stage-{role}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n snapshot.time_until_reset_hours\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        if 0 < hours <= self.WASTE_WINDOW_HOURS and remaining > 0:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            waste_urgency = remaining * (self.WASTE_WINDOW_HOURS - hours) / self.WASTE_WINDOW_HOURS\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        # Safety bonus: prefer accounts that can sustain a full task\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        safety = self.SAFETY_BONUS if remaining > self.TASK_SAFETY_THRESHOLD else 0\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        # Inertia: preference for staying on current account\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        # Extra inertia when healthy \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\u2014 don't switch between two good accounts\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        inertia = 0\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        if is_current:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            inertia = self.INERTIA_BONUS\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            if remaining > self.TASK_SAFETY_THRESHOLD:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                inertia += self.HEALTHY_INERTIA\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        return remaining + waste_urgency + safety + inertia\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def _is_fresh_reset(self, snapshot: QuotaSnapshot) -> bool:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n window.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        return snapshot.remaining_percent >= self.FRESH_QUOTA_THRESHOLD\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def analyze_and_decide(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        self,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        current_email: str,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        current_snapshot: Optional[QuotaSnapshot],\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        all_snapshots: list[QuotaSnapshot],\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    ) -> SwitchDecision:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        Analyze quota situation and decide whether to switch accounts.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        Uses unified scoring that balances:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        1. Waste prevention (use expiring quota before it's lost)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        2. Task safety (prefer >40% for new tasks)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        3. Continuity (inertia for current account)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        if not current_snapshot:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            # No current account info \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\u2014 pick the best available\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            if all_snapshots:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                scored = [(s, self._score_account(s)) for s in all_snapshots]\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                best, best_score = max(scored, key=lambda x: x[1])\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                return SwitchDecision(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    should_switch=True,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    reason=f\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"No current account \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\u2014 selecting best (score {best_score:.0f})\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                    target_email=best.email,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n available\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                target_email=None,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n current_remaining=None,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            )\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        # Score current account\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        current_score = self._score_account(current_snapshot, is_current=True)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        # Score all candidates\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        candidates = [s for s in all_snapshots if s.email != current_email]\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        if not candidates:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            return SwitchDecision(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                should_switch=False,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n = max(scored, key=lambda x: x[1])\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        improvement = best_score - current_score\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        # Determine reason for decision\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        if improvement > self.SWITCH_THRESHOLD:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            # Explain WHY we're switching\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            reason_parts = []\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n {best_hours:.1f}h\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n                )\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            if remaining)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            if self._is_fresh_reset(best_snapshot):\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n ({best_snapshot.remaining_percent}%)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n            reason = fail_fast\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nasync def ensure_servers_running(config_path: str, timeout: float = 3.0) -> bool:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n required servers are healthy. {server.base_url} is DOWN{err}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nPlease start the required servers (e.g. using scripts/run_*_server.py).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    return False\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ndef get_available_workflows() -> list[Path]:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Return a list of workflow YAML files.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    workflows_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent / \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"workflows\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    if not workflows_dir.exists():\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        return []\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    return sorted(workflows_dir.glob(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"*.yaml\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"))\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nasync def main_async(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    # 1. Check Servers\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    servers_ok = await ensure_servers_running(args.config, args.timeout)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    if not servers_ok:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"               ORCHESTRA MULTI-AGENT CLI\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"*60 + \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    # 2. Select Workflow\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    workflows = get_available_workflows()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    if not workflows:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n directory.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n foreign_keys = ON;\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    task_id TEXT PRIMARY HarnessDB:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n exist_ok=True)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n        self.initialize()\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n    def initialize(self) -> artifacts/9198447474234958b318e2ef1de5cfe2/01_plan_output.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"# Output: plan\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Summary\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nImplement the robustness work in three slices, keeping `QuotaRouter` selection pure and pushing all operational side effects into the harness runtime / CLI layer.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n1. Codex account rotation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Keep `QuotaRouter.select_model()` and `can_run_stage()` side-effect free.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Change Codex candidate selection from \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"best provider snapshot\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" to \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"best eligible Codex account snapshot\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", using the existing use-it-or-lose-it score.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Preserve the chosen `account_email` on the selected stage, preferably in stage metadata if the DB schema should stay stable.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add an explicit pre-execution Codex activation step that calls `models.codex.account.get_active_email()` and `set_active_account()` only when the chosen account differs.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n2. Daemon lifecycle\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Use the harness CLI in [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) as the management entrypoint, not the older workflow CLI in [scripts/orchestra_cli.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/scripts/orchestra_cli.py).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add `daemon start`, `daemon stop`, `daemon status`, plus a hidden worker subcommand that runs `Harness.run_forever()`.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Prefer a portable detached `subprocess.Popen(..., start_new_session=True)` / nohup-style path for tests instead of systemd-specific logic.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Persist PID metadata, detect stale PID files, and handle `SIGTERM` so the loop exits cleanly after the current iteration.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n3. Logging, monitoring, and dashboard\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add centralized logging setup for harness CLI + daemon startup.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Emit structured JSONL stage events for start, success, retry, quota pause, permission pause, and terminal failure.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Reuse persisted `stages.token_used` and `stages.duration_sec` for aggregates; parse provider output for token usage where available and leave `0` when unavailable.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add a `dashboard` command that combines DB state, quota/permission audit tables, daemon state, and recent JSONL events into one status summary.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Key File Targets\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- [harness/task_queue.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- [models/codex/account.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/models/codex/account.py)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- [config/settings.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/config/settings.py)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- New modules are justified for daemon management and telemetry aggregation.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Risks To Avoid\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Do not let quota feasibility checks mutate global Codex auth state.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Do not add daemon logic to the legacy orchestrator CLI; keep it on the harness path.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Do not rely on live systemd in tests; use deterministic unit seams around PID files, signals, and subprocess launch.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Test Plan\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Extend router tests for multi-account Codex ranking and no-side-effect feasibility checks.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add harness tests for Codex account activation before a Codex stage executes.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add daemon tests for PID lifecycle, stale PID cleanup, and graceful stop/status behavior.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add telemetry/dashboard tests Handoff: plan -> code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Task\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nPhase 4: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\u5065\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\u58ee\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\u6027 for code-orchestra harness\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Context Gathered\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- `QuotaRouter` currently collapses each provider to a single best snapshot in [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py), which is not enough for Codex multi-account rotation.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- `Harness._run_stage()` in [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) persists model/provider selection but has no Codex account activation, no daemon hooks, and no telemetry hooks.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- `models.codex.account.set_active_account()` already handles token refresh + auth file switching in [models/codex/account.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/models/codex/account.py); the harness should integrate it as a runtime step, not from the router.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- `StageExecutionResult` already has `token_used` and `duration_sec`, and the stages table already persists both via [harness/task_queue.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Baseline verification is green: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -q` => `21 passed`.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Recommended Implementation Order\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n1. Codex rotation\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Update router eligibility/fallback logic so Codex models consider all eligible Codex snapshots, keeping the chosen `account_email`.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Persist the selected account on the stage.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add a small helper in the harness path to activate the selected Codex account just before execution.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add tests first for router ranking, then for harness-side activation.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n2. Daemon mode\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add a dedicated daemon helper module with PID file read/write, process liveness checks, start/stop/status, and stale PID cleanup.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Extend the harness parser with `daemon` and a hidden worker command.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add `SIGTERM`-aware stop handling around `run_forever`, ideally with an `asyncio.Event` / shutdown flag seam that is easy to unit test.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n3. Telemetry + dashboard\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add centralized logging config and a JSONL event writer module.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Emit stage lifecycle events from the harness runtime, not from the router.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Add a dashboard summary builder that reads DB state + event log + daemon status.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Expose it via `dashboard` in the harness CLI.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Concrete Guidance\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Prefer stage metadata for the selected Codex account instead of widening the DB schema unless a wider schema clearly improves readability.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Keep `can_run_stage()` pure; it is called from paused-task recovery and should not switch accounts.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Parse token usage opportunistically in [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py); do not block this phase on provider-specific token support.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n- Treat [scripts/orchestra_cli.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/scripts/orchestra_cli.py) as unrelated legacy workflow UI unless you discover a hard requirement otherwise.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n## Verification Target\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nRun `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v` after each slice if possible, then once at the Task\\\\\\\\nPhase 4: 健壮性 for code-orchestra harness\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Context Gathered\\\\\\\\n- [harness/quota_router.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/quota_router.py) 当前按 provider 取单个最佳 quota snapshot；这会丢掉 Codex 的账号级选择空间。\\\\\\\\n- [harness/main.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/main.py) 当前只持久化 `assigned_model` / `assigned_provider`，执行前没有 Codex 账号激活，也没有 daemon / telemetry hook。\\\\\\\\n- [harness/task_queue.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/task_queue.py) 已经有 `metadata` 字段，但缺少单独的 metadata 更新接口；这是保存 `account_email` 的最小改动点。\\\\\\\\n- [harness/stage_executor.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/harness/stage_executor.py) 已经返回 `duration_sec` / `token_used`，其中 `token_used` 目前始终接近未使用状态，适合在这里补 provider 输出解析。\\\\\\\\n- [models/codex/account.py](/home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra/models/codex/account.py) 已提供 `get_active_email()` 和 `set_active_account()`；账号切换副作用应放到 runtime，而不是 router。\\\\\\\\n- 当前基线: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v` 通过，结果是 `21 passed`。\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Recommended Order\\\\\\\\n1. 先做 Codex 轮换\\\\\\\\n- router 改成账号级候选。\\\\\\\\n- stage metadata 持久化 `selected_account_email`。\\\\\\\\n- `Harness._run_stage()` 在真正执行前切换 Codex 账号。\\\\\\\\n- 先补 router 和 harness 测试，再动实现。\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n2. 再做 daemon\\\\\\\\n- 新建 daemon helper 模块，不要把 PID/信号逻辑塞满 `main.py`。\\\\\\\\n- parser 增加 `daemon` 子命令和内部 worker 子命令。\\\\\\\\n- `run_forever()` 增加可优雅退出的 stop seam。\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n3. 最后做 telemetry 和 dashboard\\\\\\\\n- 统一 logging 配置。\\\\\\\\n- stage 生命周期事件写 JSONL。\\\\\\\\n- `dashboard` 聚合 DB + daemon + recent events。\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Concrete Guidance\\\\\\\\n- `QuotaRouter.can_run_stage()` 必须保持无副作用，因为它会在 paused quota 恢复逻辑中反复调用。\\\\\\\\n- `TaskQueue.assign_stage_model()` 很可能需要扩成同时支持 metadata patch，或者新增 `update_stage_metadata()`。\\\\\\\\n- daemon 测试应 mock `Popen`、`os.kill(pid, 0)` 或等价存活检查，避免真实后台进程依赖。\\\\\\\\n- `dashboard` 不必做复杂 TUI，先输出稳定 JSON/结构化文本即可。\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Verification Target\\\\\\\\n收尾时运行: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\nfrom typing import Iterable\\\\n\\\\nfrom harness.models import StageExecutionResult, StageRecord, TaskRecord\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass HandoffProtocol:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Persist stage inputs and outputs as markdown artifacts.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, artifact_root: str | Path = \\\\\\\"artifacts\\\\\\\") -> None:\\\\n        self.artifact_root = Path(artifact_root)\\\\n        self.artifact_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\n\\\\n    def task_dir(self, task_id: str) -> Path:\\\\n        path = self.artifact_root / task_id\\\\n        path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\n        return path\\\\n\\\\n    def handoff_path(self, task: TaskRecord, stage: StageRecord) -> Path:\\\\n        return self.task_dir(task.task_id) / f\\\\\\\"{stage.stage_order:02d}_{stage.stage_type}_handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def output_path(self, task: TaskRecord, stage: StageRecord) -> Path:\\\\n        return self.task_dir(task.task_id) / f\\\\\\\"{stage.stage_order:02d}_{stage.stage_type}_output.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def generate(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        task: TaskRecord,\\\\n        stage: StageRecord,\\\\n        previous_stages: Iterable[StageRecord],\\\\n    ) -> str:\\\\n        previous = list(previous_stages)\\\\n        previous_stage_name = previous[-1].stage_type if previous else \\\\\\\"bootstrap\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n        lines = [\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"# Handoff: {previous_stage_name} -> {stage.stage_type}\\\\\\\",\\\\n            \\\\\\\"## Task\\\\\\\",\\\\n            task.title,\\\\n            \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n            \\\\\\\"## Description\\\\\\\",\\\\n            task.description,\\\\n            \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n        ]\\\\n\\\\n        if task.goal:\\\\n            lines.extend([\\\\\\\"## Goal\\\\\\\", task.goal, \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"])\\\\n\\\\n        lines.extend(\\\\n            [\\\\n                \\\\\\\"## Context\\\\\\\",\\\\n                f\\\\\\\"- Complexity: {task.complexity}\\\\\\\",\\\\n                f\\\\\\\"- Current Stage: {stage.stage_type} ({stage.model_role})\\\\\\\",\\\\n                f\\\\\\\"- Working Directory: {task.working_dir or '.'}\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n            ]\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n        if previous:\\\\n            lines.append(\\\\\\\"## Previous Stage Output\\\\\\\")\\\\n            for previous_stage in previous:\\\\n                summary = previous_stage.result_summary or \\\\\\\"No summary recorded.\\\\\\\"\\\\n                lines.extend(\\\\n                    [\\\\n                        f\\\\\\\"### {previous_stage.stage_type}\\\\\\\",\\\\n                        summary.strip(),\\\\n                        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                    ]\\\\n                )\\\\n\\\\n        lines.append(\\\\\\\"## Current Stage Instructions\\\\\\\")\\\\n        instruction = stage.metadata.get(\\\\\\\"instructions\\\\\\\") if stage.metadata else None\\\\n        if instruction:\\\\n            lines.append(str(instruction).strip())\\\\n        else:\\\\n            lines.append(\\\\n                f\\\\\\\"Complete the `{stage.stage_type}` stage for this task in `{task.working_dir or '.'}`.\\\\\\\"\\\\n            )\\\\n -lc \\\"sed -n '1,140p' harness/db.py\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\\\"\\\"\\\"SQLite schema and connection helpers for the harness.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport sqlite3\\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\nfrom typing import Iterator\\n\\n\\nSCHEMA = \\\"\\\"\\\"\\nPRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\\n\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (\\n    task_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n    title TEXT NOT NULL,\\n    description TEXT NOT NULL,\\n    goal TEXT,\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\n    complexity TEXT DEFAULT 'medium',\\n    priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\n    working_dir TEXT,\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,\\n    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL\\n);\\n\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stages (\\n    stage_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n    task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(task_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\n    stage_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\n    stage_order INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n    model_role TEXT NOT NULL,\\n    assigned_model TEXT,\\n    assigned_provider TEXT,\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\n    handoff_doc_path TEXT,\\n    result_summary TEXT,\\n    token_used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\n    duration_sec REAL DEFAULT 0,\\n    started_at TEXT,\\n    finished_at TEXT,\\n    retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\n    metadata TEXT DEFAULT '{}'\\n);\\n\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS permission_requests (\\n    request_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n    stage_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES stages(stage_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\n    action TEXT NOT NULL,\\n    context TEXT,\\n    risk_level TEXT NOT NULL,\\n    decision TEXT,\\n    voters TEXT DEFAULT '[]',\\n    decided_at TEXT\\n);\\n\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quota_events (\\n    event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n    provider TEXT NOT NULL,\\n    account_email TEXT,\\n    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\n    details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL\\n);\\n\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status_priority\\n    ON tasks(status, priority DESC, created_at ASC);\\n\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_order\\n    ON stages(task_id, stage_order ASC);\\n\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_status\\n    ON stages(task_id, status, stage_order ASC);\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n\\nclass HarnessDB:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Small wrapper around a SQLite database file.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def __init__(self, db_path: str | Path) -> None:\\n        self.db_path = Path(db_path)\\n        self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n        self.initialize()\\n\\n    def initialize(self) -> None:\\n        with self.connect() as conn:\\n            conn.executescript(SCHEMA)\\n\\n    @contextmanager\\n    def connect(self) -> Iterator[sqlite3.Connection]:\\n        conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)\\n        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\\n        conn.execute(\\\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON\\\")\\n        try:\\n            yield conn\\n            conn.commit()\\n        except Exception:\\n            conn.rollback()\\n            raise\\n        finally:\\n executor = StageExecutor(\n+        permission_gate=DummyPermissionGate(\n+            PermissionDecision(risk_level=RiskLevel.SAFE, decision=\"auto_approved\", reason=\"ok\")\n+        ),\n+        runner=_runner,\n+    )\n+    cmd = executor.build_command(make_gemini_stage(), \"Create hello.py\", str(tmp_path))\n+    assert \"-y\" in cmd, f\"Missing -y (yolo) flag in Gemini command: {cmd}\"\n+\n+\n+def test_gemini_command_has_prompt_flag(tmp_path) -> None:\n+    \"\"\"Gemini CLI MUST have -p flag for non-interactive headless mode.\"\"\"\n+    executor = StageExecutor(\n+        permission_gate=DummyPermissionGate(\n+            PermissionDecision(risk_level=RiskLevel.SAFE, decision=\"auto_approved\", reason=\"ok\")\n+        ),\n+        runner=_runner,\n+    )\n+    cmd = executor.build_command(make_gemini_stage(), \"Create hello.py\", str(tmp_path))\n+    assert \"-p\" in cmd, f\"Missing -p flag in Gemini command: {cmd}\"\n+    # -p must come before the prompt text\n+    p_idx = cmd.index(\"-p\")\n+    assert cmd[p_idx + 1] == \"Create hello.py\"\n+\n+\n+def test_gemini_command_uses_correct_model(tmp_path) -> None:\n+    executor = StageExecutor(\n+        permission_gate=DummyPermissionGate(\n+            PermissionDecision(risk_level=RiskLevel.SAFE, decision=\"auto_approved\", reason=\"ok\")\n+        ),\n+        runner=_runner,\n+    )\n+    cmd = executor.build_command(make_gemini_stage(\"gemini-2.5-pro\"), \"test\", str(tmp_path))\n+    model_idx = cmd.index(\"--model\")\n+    assert cmd[model_idx + 1] == \"gemini-2.5-pro\"\n+\n+\n+def test_gemini_command_flag_order(tmp_path) -> None:\n+    \"\"\"Ensure flags are in correct order: gemini --model X -y -p PROMPT\"\"\"\n+    executor = StageExecutor(\n+        permission_gate=DummyPermissionGate(\n+            PermissionDecision(risk_level=RiskLevel.SAFE, decision=\"auto_approved\", reason=\"ok\")\n+        ),\n+        runner=_runner,\n+    )\n+    cmd = executor.build_command(make_gemini_stage(), \"do stuff\", str(tmp_path))\n+    assert cmd[0] == \"gemini\"\n+    assert cmd[1] == \"--model\"\n+    # -y must be before -p (yolo must be set before prompt is processed)\n+    y_idx = cmd.index(\"-y\")\n+    p_idx = cmd.index(\"-p\")\n+    assert y_idx < p_idx, f\"-y (idx={y_idx}) must come before -p (idx={p_idx})\"\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_12","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' harness/model_registry.py\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_12","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' harness/model_registry.py\"","aggregated_output":"\"\"\"Unified model name resolution.\n\nEvery model has one canonical name (the key in role_models.yaml).\nEach model may declare aliases — alternative names used by chat2api,\nCLI tools, or shorthand references.\n\nModelRegistry enforces a strict invariant: **no alias may point to\nmore than one canonical name**.  This is validated at load time so\nthat name collisions are caught early rather than at runtime.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport yaml\n\nfrom config import settings\n\n\nclass AliasCollisionError(ValueError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when an alias resolves to more than one canonical model.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass ModelRegistry:\n    \"\"\"Single source of truth for model name resolution.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, catalog_path: str | Path = settings.HARNESS_ROLE_MODELS_PATH) -> None:\n        self._catalog = self._load(catalog_path)\n        self._models: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = self._catalog.get(\"models\", {})\n        # canonical name → set of aliases (including the canonical name itself)\n        self._alias_to_canonical: dict[str, str] = {}\n        self._build_index()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Public API\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def resolve(self, name: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Resolve any name (canonical or alias) to its canonical name.\n\n        Returns the input unchanged if it is not recognised — this keeps\n        the system open to ad-hoc model names that haven't been catalogued.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self._alias_to_canonical.get(name, name)\n\n    def chat2api_id(self, name: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the chat2api model ID for *name* (canonical or alias).\"\"\"\n        canonical = self.resolve(name)\n        info = self._models.get(canonical, {})\n        return info.get(\"chat2api_id\", canonical)\n\n    def cli_model_id(self, name: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the CLI model ID for *name* (canonical or alias).\n\n        Falls back to the canonical name if no cli_model_id is configured.\n        \"\"\"\n        canonical = self.resolve(name)\n        info = self._models.get(canonical, {})\n        return info.get(\"cli_model_id\", canonical)\n\n    def provider(self, name: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the provider string for *name*.\"\"\"\n        canonical = self.resolve(name)\n        return self._models.get(canonical, {}).get(\"provider\", \"\")\n\n    def get_info(self, name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Return the full catalog entry for *name*.\"\"\"\n        canonical = self.resolve(name)\n        return dict(self._models.get(canonical, {}))\n\n    def canonical_names(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return all canonical model names.\"\"\"\n        return list(self._models.keys())\n\n    def available_canonical_names(self, live_ids: set[str]) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return canonical names whose chat2api_id appears in *live_ids*.\"\"\"\n        return [\n            name for name in self._models\n            if self.chat2api_id(name) in live_ids\n        ]\n\n    def roles(self) -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n        \"\"\"Return role → [canonical model names] mapping.\"\"\"\n        return dict(self._catalog.get(\"roles\", {}))\n\n    def models_for_role(self, role: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return ordered candidate list for *role*.\"\"\"\n        roles = self._catalog.get(\"roles\", {})\n        configured = roles.get(role)\n        if configured:\n            return list(configured)\n        return self.canonical_names()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internals\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _load(path: str | Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        with open(path, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as fh:\n            return yaml.safe_load(fh) or {}\n\n    def _build_index(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Build alias → canonical mapping with collision detection.\n\n        Only the canonical name and explicit aliases are registered.\n        chat2api_id and cli_model_id are *not* registered as aliases\n        because multiple models may share a gateway ID (e.g. sonnet\n        and haiku both use ``copilot-claude``).  Outbound resolution\n        uses the dedicated ``chat2api_id()`` / ``cli_model_id()``\n        methods which look up the value directly.\n        \"\"\"\n        for canonical, info in self._models.items():\n            self._register(canonical, canonical)\n            for alias in info.get(\"aliases\", []):\n                if alias and alias != canonical:\n                    self._register(alias, canonical)\n\n    def _register(self, alias: str, canonical: str) -> None:\n        existing = self._alias_to_canonical.get(alias)\n        if existing is not None and existing != canonical:\n            raise AliasCollisionError(\n                f\"Alias {alias!r} maps to both {existing!r} and {canonical!r}. \"\n                f\"Each alias must resolve to exactly one canonical model.\"\n            )\n        self._alias_to_canonical[alias] = canonical\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_13","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' harness/main.py\"","aggregated_output":"\"\"\"Harness entry point and scheduler loop.\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport signal\nimport sys\nimport uuid\nfrom collections import Counter\nfrom dataclasses import asdict\nfrom dataclasses import replace\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom config import settings\nfrom harness.chat2api_client import Chat2APIClient\nfrom harness.checkpoint import CheckpointStore\nfrom harness.codex_runtime import CodexAccountRuntime\nfrom harness.daemon import DaemonManager\nfrom harness.db import HarnessDB\nfrom harness.handoff import HandoffProtocol\nfrom harness.intake import IntakeAgent\nfrom harness.logging_utils import configure_logging\nfrom harness.model_registry import ModelRegistry\nfrom harness.models import (\n    IntakeResult,\n    PermissionRequestRecord,\n    QuotaEventRecord,\n    StageCheckpoint,\n    StageRecord,\n    StageStatus,\n    TaskRecord,\n    TaskStatus,\n)\nfrom harness.permission_gate import PermissionGate\nfrom harness.quota_router import AllQuotaExhaustedError, QuotaRouter\nfrom harness.stage_executor import PermissionBlockedError, QuotaExhaustedError, StageExecutionError, StageExecutor\nfrom harness.task_queue import TaskQueue, utcnow_iso\nfrom harness.telemetry import HarnessTelemetry\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass Harness:\n    \"\"\"Main scheduler that turns queued tasks into stage executions.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        db_path: str | Path = settings.HARNESS_DB_PATH,\n        artifact_root: str | Path = settings.HARNESS_ARTIFACT_DIR,\n        intake_agent: Optional[IntakeAgent] = None,\n        quota_router: Optional[QuotaRouter] = None,\n        stage_executor: Optional[StageExecutor] = None,\n        telemetry: Optional[HarnessTelemetry] = None,\n        codex_runtime: Optional[CodexAccountRuntime] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.db = HarnessDB(db_path)\n        self.task_queue = TaskQueue(self.db)\n        self.handoff = HandoffProtocol(artifact_root)\n        self.checkpoints = CheckpointStore(artifact_root)\n        self.intake = intake_agent or IntakeAgent()\n        self.router = quota_router or QuotaRouter()\n        self.executor = stage_executor or StageExecutor()\n        self.telemetry = telemetry or HarnessTelemetry()\n        self.codex_runtime = codex_runtime or CodexAccountRuntime()\n\n    def submit_task(\n        self,\n        description: str,\n        *,\n        title: Optional[str] = None,\n        goal: Optional[str] = None,\n        verify_cmd: Optional[str] = None,\n        priority: int = 50,\n        working_dir: Optional[str] = None,\n        model_overrides: Optional[dict[str, tuple[str, str]]] = None,\n    ) -> TaskRecord:\n        intake = self.intake.plan_task(\n            description, goal=goal, verify_cmd=verify_cmd, model_overrides=model_overrides\n        )\n        if title:\n            intake = replace(intake, title=title)\n        return self.submit_intake(intake, priority=priority, working_dir=working_dir)\n\n    def submit_intake(\n        self,\n        intake: IntakeResult,\n        *,\n        priority: int = 50,\n        working_dir: Optional[str] = None,\n        status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.EXECUTING,\n    ) -> TaskRecord:\n        task = self.task_queue.create_task(\n            title=intake.title,\n            description=intake.description,\n            goal=intake.goal,\n            verify_cmd=intake.verify_cmd,\n            complexity=intake.complexity,\n            priority=priority,\n            status=status,\n            working_dir=working_dir,\n        )\n        self.task_queue.save_stages(task.task_id, intake.stages)\n        return task\n\n    async def run_once(self) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\n        await self._recover_paused_quota_tasks()\n        task = self.task_queue.pick_next_runnable_task()\n        if task is None:\n            return None\n\n        if self.task_queue.count_stages(task.task_id) == 0:\n            await self._plan_missing_stages(task)\n            task = self.task_queue.get_task(task.task_id) or task\n\n        stage = self.task_queue.next_pending_stage(task.task_id)\n        if stage is None:\n            await self._finish_task(task)\n            return self.task_queue.get_task(task.task_id)\n\n        await self._run_stage(task, stage)\n        return self.task_queue.get_task(task.task_id)\n\n    async def run_forever(\n        self,\n        poll_interval_sec: int = settings.HARNESS_POLL_INTERVAL_SEC,\n        *,\n        stop_event: Optional[asyncio.Event] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        while stop_event is None or not stop_event.is_set():\n            task = await self.run_once()\n            if task is None:\n                if stop_event is None:\n                    await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval_sec)\n                else:\n                    try:\n                        await asyncio.wait_for(stop_event.wait(), timeout=poll_interval_sec)\n                    except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                        pass\n\n    def status_report(self) -> list[dict[str, object]]:\n        report: list[dict[str, object]] = []\n        for task in self.task_queue.list_tasks():\n            report.append(\n                {\n                    \"task_id\": task.task_id,\n                    \"title\": task.title,\n                    \"status\": task.status.value,\n                    \"priority\": task.priority,\n                    \"complexity\": task.complexity,\n                    \"working_dir\": task.working_dir,\n                    \"stages\": [\n                        {\n                            \"stage_type\": stage.stage_type,\n                            \"status\": stage.status.value,\n                            \"assigned_model\": stage.assigned_model,\n                            \"retry_count\": stage.retry_count,\n                        }\n                        for stage in self.task_queue.list_stages(task.task_id)\n                    ],\n                }\n            )\n        return report\n\n    def inspect_task(self, task_id: str) -> dict[str, object]:\n        task = self.task_queue.get_task(task_id)\n        if task is None:\n            raise KeyError(f\"Task not found: {task_id}\")\n\n        stages = self.task_queue.list_stages(task_id)\n        return {\n            \"task\": {\n                \"task_id\": task.task_id,\n                \"title\": task.title,\n                \"description\": task.description,\n                \"goal\": task.goal,\n                \"verify_cmd\": task.verify_cmd,\n                \"status\": task.status.value,\n                \"priority\": task.priority,\n                \"complexity\": task.complexity,\n                \"working_dir\": task.working_dir,\n                \"created_at\": task.created_at,\n                \"updated_at\": task.updated_at,\n            },\n            \"stages\": [\n                {\n                    \"stage_id\": stage.stage_id,\n                    \"stage_type\": stage.stage_type,\n                    \"model_role\": stage.model_role,\n                    \"status\": stage.status.value,\n                    \"assigned_model\": stage.assigned_model,\n                    \"assigned_provider\": stage.assigned_provider,\n                    \"handoff_doc_path\": stage.handoff_doc_path,\n                    \"result_summary\": stage.result_summary,\n                    \"retry_count\": stage.retry_count,\n                    \"verify_cmd\": stage.verify_cmd,\n                    \"metadata\": stage.metadata,\n                }\n                for stage in stages\n            ],\n            \"permission_requests\": [\n                {\n                    \"request_id\": item.request_id,\n                    \"stage_id\": item.stage_id,\n                    \"action\": item.action,\n                    \"risk_level\": item.risk_level.value,\n                    \"decision\": item.decision,\n                    \"voters\": item.voters,\n                    \"decided_at\": item.decided_at,\n                }\n                for stage in stages\n                for item in self.task_queue.list_permission_requests(stage.stage_id)\n            ],\n            \"checkpoints\": [\n                asdict(checkpoint) for checkpoint in self.checkpoints.list_for_task(task_id)\n            ],\n        }\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_14","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' tests/harness/test_intake.py\"","aggregated_output":"from harness.intake import IntakeAgent\n\n\ndef test_intake_plans_simple_task() -> None:\n    agent = IntakeAgent()\n\n    result = agent.plan_task(\n        \"Add a blacklist setting to the scheduler, wire config loading, and cover it with tests.\"\n    )\n\n    assert result.title == \"Add a blacklist setting to the scheduler, wire config loading, and cover it\"\n    assert result.complexity == \"simple\"\n    assert [stage.stage_type for stage in result.stages] == [\"code\", \"review\", \"github_ops\"]\n    assert [stage.model_role for stage in result.stages] == [\"coder\", \"reviewer\", \"github_ops\"]\n\n\ndef test_intake_marks_complex_work() -> None:\n    agent = IntakeAgent()\n\n    result = agent.plan_task(\n        \"Build a quota-aware scheduler with checkpoint recovery, permission voting, \"\n        \"workflow persistence, and a resumable execution loop.\"\n    )\n\n    assert result.complexity == \"complex\"\n    assert [stage.stage_type for stage in result.stages] == [\n        \"plan\",\n        \"code\",\n        \"review\",\n        \"test\",\n        \"e2e_test\",\n        \"github_ops\",\n    ]\n\n\ndef test_intake_generates_questions_and_applies_answers() -> None:\n    agent = IntakeAgent()\n    description = \"Add a blacklist setting for subreddit filtering.\"\n\n    questions = agent.generate_questions(description, \"simple\")\n    result = agent.apply_answers(\n        description,\n        {\n            \"storage\": \"config/blacklist.yaml\",\n            \"verify_cmd\": \"pytest tests/test_blacklist.py\",\n            \"needs_cli\": \"y\",\n        },\n    )\n\n    assert [question.key for question in questions] == [\"storage\"]\n    assert \"Storage preference: config/blacklist.yaml.\" in result.description\n    assert \"User requested a CLI or management entrypoint.\" in result.description\n    assert result.verify_cmd == \"pytest tests/test_blacklist.py\"\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_15","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' tests/harness/test_main.py\"","aggregated_output":"import asyncio\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom harness.main import Harness\nfrom harness.models import ModelChoice, PlannedStage, StageExecutionResult, StageStatus, TaskStatus\nfrom harness.stage_executor import CommandExecution, StageExecutionError\nfrom harness.telemetry import HarnessTelemetry\n\n\nclass DummyRouter:\n    def select_model(self, task, stage, **kwargs):\n        return ModelChoice(model=\"gpt-5.4-codex\", provider=\"codex\", reason=\"test\")\n\n    def can_run_stage(self, task, stage, **kwargs):\n        return True\n\n\nclass DummyExecutor:\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self.executed = []\n\n    async def execute(self, stage, handoff_doc_path, working_dir):\n        self.executed.append((stage.stage_type, handoff_doc_path, working_dir))\n        return StageExecutionResult(\n            stage_id=stage.stage_id,\n            status=StageStatus.DONE,\n            raw_output=\"implemented feature\",\n            summary=\"implemented feature\",\n            files_changed=[\"src/feature.py\"],\n        )\n\n    async def run_verify_cmd(self, verify_cmd, working_dir):\n        return CommandExecution(returncode=0, stdout=\"ok\", stderr=\"\")\n\n\nclass DummyCodexRuntime:\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self.calls = []\n\n    def ensure_active(self, account_email):\n        self.calls.append(account_email)\n        return True\n\n\nclass AccountSelectingRouter(DummyRouter):\n    def select_model(self, task, stage, **kwargs):\n        return ModelChoice(\n            model=\"gpt-5.4-codex\",\n            provider=\"codex\",\n            account_email=\"codex-2@test\",\n            reason=\"test-account-rotation\",\n        )\n\n\nclass FlakyExecutor(DummyExecutor):\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.calls = 0\n\n    async def execute(self, stage, handoff_doc_path, working_dir):\n        self.calls += 1\n        if self.calls == 1:\n            raise StageExecutionError(\"temporary failure\")\n        return StageExecutionResult(\n            stage_id=stage.stage_id,\n            status=StageStatus.DONE,\n            raw_output=\"implemented feature\",\n            summary=\"implemented feature\",\n            files_changed=[\"src/feature.py\"],\n            duration_sec=4.5,\n            token_used=321,\n        )\n\n\ndef test_harness_run_once_executes_stage_and_finishes_task(tmp_path) -> None:\n    executor = DummyExecutor()\n    harness = Harness(\n        db_path=tmp_path / \"harness.db\",\n        artifact_root=tmp_path / \"artifacts\",\n        quota_router=DummyRouter(),\n        stage_executor=executor,\n        telemetry=HarnessTelemetry(tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\"),\n    )\n    task = harness.task_queue.create_task(\n        \"task\",\n        \"desc\",\n        status=TaskStatus.EXECUTING,\n        working_dir=str(tmp_path),\n    )\n    harness.task_queue.save_stages(\n        task.task_id,\n        [PlannedStage(stage_type=\"code\", stage_order=1, model_role=\"coder\")],\n    )\n\n    asyncio.run(harness.run_once())\n\n    updated_task = harness.task_queue.get_task(task.task_id)\n    [stage] = harness.task_queue.list_stages(task.task_id)\n\n    assert updated_task is not None\n    assert updated_task.status == TaskStatus.DONE\n    assert stage.status == StageStatus.DONE\n    assert stage.handoff_doc_path is not None\n    assert Path(stage.handoff_doc_path).exists()\n    assert executor.executed == [(\"code\", stage.handoff_doc_path, str(tmp_path))]\n\n\ndef test_harness_recovers_paused_quota_tasks(tmp_path) -> None:\n    executor = DummyExecutor()\n    harness = Harness(\n        db_path=tmp_path / \"harness.db\",\n        artifact_root=tmp_path / \"artifacts\",\n        quota_router=DummyRouter(),\n        stage_executor=executor,\n        telemetry=HarnessTelemetry(tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\"),\n    )\n    task = harness.task_queue.create_task(\n        \"task\",\n        \"desc\",\n        status=TaskStatus.PAUSED_QUOTA,\n        working_dir=str(tmp_path),\n    )\n    harness.task_queue.save_stages(\n        task.task_id,\n        [PlannedStage(stage_type=\"code\", stage_order=1, model_role=\"coder\")],\n    )\n\n    asyncio.run(harness.run_once())\n\n    updated_task = harness.task_queue.get_task(task.task_id)\n    assert updated_task is not None\n    assert updated_task.status == TaskStatus.DONE\n\n\ndef test_harness_inspect_includes_permission_requests(tmp_path) -> None:\n    executor = DummyExecutor()\n    harness = Harness(\n        db_path=tmp_path / \"harness.db\",\n        artifact_root=tmp_path / \"artifacts\",\n        quota_router=DummyRouter(),\n        stage_executor=executor,\n        telemetry=HarnessTelemetry(tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\"),\n    )\n    task = harness.task_queue.create_task(\"task\", \"desc\", status=TaskStatus.EXECUTING, working_dir=str(tmp_path))\n    [stage] = harness.task_queue.save_stages(\n        task.task_id,\n        [PlannedStage(stage_type=\"code\", stage_order=1, model_role=\"coder\")],\n    )\n    from harness.models import PermissionRequestRecord, RiskLevel\n\n    harness.task_queue.log_permission_request(\n        PermissionRequestRecord(\n            request_id=\"req-1\",\n            stage_id=stage.stage_id,\n            action=\"git push origin main\",\n            context=\"task=demo\",\n            risk_level=RiskLevel.DANGEROUS,\n            decision=\"needs_user_approval\",\n            voters=[{\"model\": \"gpt-5.4-codex\", \"vote\": \"REJECT\", \"reason\": \"too risky\"}],\n            decided_at=\"2026-03-27T00:00:00+00:00\",\n        )\n    )\n\n    data = harness.inspect_task(task.task_id)\n\n    assert data[\"task\"][\"task_id\"] == task.task_id\n    assert data[\"permission_requests\"][0][\"action\"] == \"git push origin main\"\n\n\ndef test_harness_switches_codex_account_before_execution(tmp_path) -> None:\n    executor = DummyExecutor()\n    codex_runtime = DummyCodexRuntime()\n    harness = Harness(\n        db_path=tmp_path / \"harness.db\",\n        artifact_root=tmp_path / \"artifacts\",\n        quota_router=AccountSelectingRouter(),\n        stage_executor=executor,\n        telemetry=HarnessTelemetry(tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\"),\n        codex_runtime=codex_runtime,\n    )\n    task = harness.task_queue.create_task(\n        \"task\",\n        \"desc\",\n        status=TaskStatus.EXECUTING,\n        working_dir=str(tmp_path),\n    )\n    harness.task_queue.save_stages(\n        task.task_id,\n        [PlannedStage(stage_type=\"code\", stage_order=1, model_role=\"coder\")],\n    )\n\n    asyncio.run(harness.run_once())\n\n    [stage] = harness.task_queue.list_stages(task.task_id)\n    assert codex_runtime.calls == [\"codex-2@test\"]\n    assert stage.metadata[\"selected_account_email\"] == \"codex-2@test\"\n\n\ndef test_harness_dashboard_summarizes_stage_metrics(tmp_path) -> None:\n    executor = FlakyExecutor()\n    telemetry = HarnessTelemetry(tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\")\n    harness = Harness(\n        db_path=tmp_path / \"harness.db\",\n        artifact_root=tmp_path / \"artifacts\",\n        quota_router=DummyRouter(),\n        stage_executor=executor,\n        telemetry=telemetry,\n    )\n    task = harness.task_queue.create_task(\n        \"task\",\n        \"desc\",\n        status=TaskStatus.EXECUTING,\n        working_dir=str(tmp_path),\n    )\n    harness.task_queue.save_stages(\n        task.task_id,\n        [PlannedStage(stage_type=\"code\", stage_order=1, model_role=\"coder\")],\n    )\n\n    asyncio.run(harness.run_once())\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_16","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' tests/harness/test_task_queue.py\"","aggregated_output":"from harness.db import HarnessDB\nfrom harness.models import PlannedStage, TaskStatus\nfrom harness.task_queue import TaskQueue\n\n\ndef test_task_queue_creates_and_orders_tasks(tmp_path) -> None:\n    queue = TaskQueue(HarnessDB(tmp_path / \"harness.db\"))\n\n    low = queue.create_task(\"low\", \"low priority task\", priority=10, status=TaskStatus.PENDING)\n    high = queue.create_task(\"high\", \"high priority task\", priority=90, status=TaskStatus.EXECUTING)\n    queue.save_stages(high.task_id, [PlannedStage(stage_type=\"code\", stage_order=1, model_role=\"coder\")])\n    queue.save_stages(low.task_id, [PlannedStage(stage_type=\"code\", stage_order=1, model_role=\"coder\")])\n\n    picked = queue.pick_next_runnable_task()\n\n    assert picked is not None\n    assert picked.task_id == high.task_id\n    assert queue.next_pending_stage(high.task_id).stage_type == \"code\"\n\n\ndef test_task_queue_updates_stage_lifecycle(tmp_path) -> None:\n    queue = TaskQueue(HarnessDB(tmp_path / \"harness.db\"))\n    task = queue.create_task(\"task\", \"desc\", status=TaskStatus.EXECUTING)\n    [stage] = queue.save_stages(\n        task.task_id,\n        [\n            PlannedStage(\n                stage_type=\"code\",\n                stage_order=1,\n                model_role=\"coder\",\n                verify_cmd=\"pytest tests/test_feature.py\",\n            )\n        ],\n    )\n\n    queue.assign_stage_model(\n        stage.stage_id,\n        \"gpt-5.4-codex\",\n        \"codex\",\n        account_email=\"codex-2@test\",\n        reason=\"use-it-or-lose-it\",\n    )\n    queue.mark_stage_running(stage.stage_id, \"/tmp/handoff.md\")\n    queue.complete_stage(stage.stage_id, result_summary=\"Implemented feature.\", token_used=123, duration_sec=4.5)\n\n    loaded = queue.get_stage(stage.stage_id)\n    assert loaded is not None\n    assert loaded.assigned_model == \"gpt-5.4-codex\"\n    assert loaded.handoff_doc_path == \"/tmp/handoff.md\"\n    assert loaded.result_summary == \"Implemented feature.\"\n    assert loaded.token_used == 123\n    assert loaded.duration_sec == 4.5\n    assert loaded.metadata[\"selected_account_email\"] == \"codex-2@test\"\n    assert loaded.metadata[\"selection_reason\"] == \"use-it-or-lose-it\"\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_17","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' harness/task_queue.py\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_17","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' harness/task_queue.py\"","aggregated_output":"\"\"\"SQLite-backed task and stage queue.\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom typing import Any, Iterable, Optional\n\nfrom harness.db import HarnessDB\nfrom harness.models import (\n    PermissionRequestRecord,\n    PlannedStage,\n    QuotaEventRecord,\n    RiskLevel,\n    StageRecord,\n    StageStatus,\n    TaskRecord,\n    TaskStatus,\n)\n\n\ndef utcnow_iso() -> str:\n    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\n\n\nclass TaskQueue:\n    \"\"\"CRUD layer for tasks, stages, and audit tables.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, db: HarnessDB) -> None:\n        self.db = db\n\n    def create_task(\n        self,\n        title: str,\n        description: str,\n        *,\n        goal: Optional[str] = None,\n        verify_cmd: Optional[str] = None,\n        complexity: str = \"medium\",\n        priority: int = 50,\n        status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.PENDING,\n        working_dir: Optional[str] = None,\n        task_id: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> TaskRecord:\n        now = utcnow_iso()\n        record = TaskRecord(\n            task_id=task_id or uuid.uuid4().hex,\n            title=title,\n            description=description,\n            goal=goal,\n            verify_cmd=verify_cmd,\n            complexity=complexity,\n            priority=priority,\n            status=status,\n            working_dir=working_dir,\n            created_at=now,\n            updated_at=now,\n        )\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n                INSERT INTO tasks (\n                    task_id, title, description, goal, verify_cmd, complexity,\n                    priority, status, working_dir, created_at, updated_at\n                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n                \"\"\",\n                (\n                    record.task_id,\n                    record.title,\n                    record.description,\n                    record.goal,\n                    record.verify_cmd,\n                    record.complexity,\n                    record.priority,\n                    record.status.value,\n                    record.working_dir,\n                    record.created_at,\n                    record.updated_at,\n                ),\n            )\n        return record\n\n    def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            row = conn.execute(\"SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE task_id = ?\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\n        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\n\n    def list_tasks(self, status: Optional[TaskStatus] = None) -> list[TaskRecord]:\n        query = \"SELECT * FROM tasks\"\n        params: tuple[object, ...] = ()\n        if status is not None:\n            query += \" WHERE status = ?\"\n            params = (status.value,)\n        query += \" ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC\"\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\n        return [self._row_to_task(row) for row in rows]\n\n    def pick_next_runnable_task(self) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            row = conn.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n                SELECT * FROM tasks\n                WHERE status IN (?, ?, ?)\n                ORDER BY\n                    CASE status\n                        WHEN ? THEN 0\n                        WHEN ? THEN 1\n                        ELSE 2\n                    END,\n                    priority DESC,\n                    created_at ASC\n                LIMIT 1\n                \"\"\",\n                (\n                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\n                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\n                    TaskStatus.PLANNING.value,\n                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\n                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\n                ),\n            ).fetchone()\n        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\n\n    def update_task_status(self, task_id: str, status: TaskStatus) -> None:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(\n                \"UPDATE tasks SET status = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\",\n                (status.value, utcnow_iso(), task_id),\n            )\n\n    def update_task_working_dir(self, task_id: str, working_dir: str) -> None:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            conn.execute(\n                \"UPDATE tasks SET working_dir = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\",\n                (working_dir, utcnow_iso(), task_id),\n            )\n\n    def save_stages(self, task_id: str, stages: Iterable[PlannedStage]) -> list[StageRecord]:\n        now = utcnow_iso()\n        records: list[StageRecord] = []\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            for planned in stages:\n                record = StageRecord(\n                    stage_id=uuid.uuid4().hex,\n                    task_id=task_id,\n                    stage_type=planned.stage_type,\n                    stage_order=planned.stage_order,\n                    model_role=planned.model_role,\n                    assigned_model=planned.assigned_model,\n                    assigned_provider=planned.assigned_provider,\n                    status=StageStatus.PENDING,\n                    handoff_doc_path=None,\n                    result_summary=None,\n                    token_used=0,\n                    duration_sec=0.0,\n                    started_at=None,\n                    finished_at=None,\n                    retry_count=0,\n                    verify_cmd=planned.verify_cmd,\n                    metadata=dict(planned.metadata),\n                )\n                conn.execute(\n                    \"\"\"\n                    INSERT INTO stages (\n                        stage_id, task_id, stage_type, stage_order, model_role,\n                        assigned_model, assigned_provider, status, handoff_doc_path,\n                        result_summary, token_used, duration_sec, started_at,\n                        finished_at, retry_count, verify_cmd, metadata\n                    ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n                    \"\"\",\n                    (\n                        record.stage_id,\n                        record.task_id,\n                        record.stage_type,\n                        record.stage_order,\n                        record.model_role,\n                        record.assigned_model,\n                        record.assigned_provider,\n                        record.status.value,\n                        record.handoff_doc_path,\n                        record.result_summary,\n                        record.token_used,\n                        record.duration_sec,\n                        record.started_at,\n                        record.finished_at,\n                        record.retry_count,\n                        record.verify_cmd,\n                        json.dumps(record.metadata),\n                    ),\n                )\n                records.append(record)\n            conn.execute(\n                \"UPDATE tasks SET updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\",\n                (now, task_id),\n            )\n        return records\n\n    def count_stages(self, task_id: str) -> int:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            row = conn.execute(\"SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM stages WHERE task_id = ?\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\n        return int(row[\"count\"])\n\n    def get_stage(self, stage_id: str) -> Optional[StageRecord]:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            row = conn.execute(\"SELECT * FROM stages WHERE stage_id = ?\", (stage_id,)).fetchone()\n        return self._row_to_stage(row) if row else None\n\n    def list_stages(self, task_id: str) -> list[StageRecord]:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            rows = conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM stages WHERE task_id = ? ORDER BY stage_order ASC\",\n                (task_id,),\n            ).fetchall()\n        return [self._row_to_stage(row) for row in rows]\n\n    def list_completed_stages(self, task_id: str) -> list[StageRecord]:\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\n            rows = conn.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

  for item in quota_events[-5:]\n                ],\n            },\n            \"permissions\": {\n                \"request_count\": len(permission_requests),\n                \"recent\": [\n                    {\n                        \"stage_id\": item.stage_id,\n                        \"action\": item.action,\n                        \"decision\": item.decision,\n                        \"risk_level\": item.risk_level.value,\n                        \"decided_at\": item.decided_at,\n                    }\n                    for item in permission_requests[-5:]\n                ],\n            },\n            \"recent_events\": telemetry[\"recent_events\"],\n        }\n\n    async def _plan_missing_stages(self, task: TaskRecord) -> None:\n        self.task_queue.update_task_status(task.task_id, TaskStatus.PLANNING)\n        intake = self.intake.plan_task(task.description, goal=task.goal, verify_cmd=task.verify_cmd)\n        self.task_queue.save_stages(task.task_id, intake.stages)\n        self.task_queue.update_task_status(task.task_id, TaskStatus.EXECUTING)\n\n    async def _recover_paused_quota_tasks(self) -> None:\n        for task in self.task_queue.list_tasks(TaskStatus.PAUSED_QUOTA):\n            stage = self.task_queue.next_pending_stage(task.task_id)\n            if stage is None:\n                continue\n            if self.router.can_run_stage(task, stage):\n                self.task_queue.update_task_status(task.task_id, TaskStatus.EXECUTING)\n                self.task_queue.log_quota_event(\n                    QuotaEventRecord(\n                        event_id=uuid.uuid4().hex,\n                        provider=stage.assigned_provider or \"unknown\",\n                        account_email=None,\n                        event_type=\"recovered\",\n                        details={\"task_id\": task.task_id, \"stage_id\": stage.stage_id},\n                        created_at=utcnow_iso(),\n                    )\n                )\n                self.telemetry.emit(\n                    \"task_resumed_quota\",\n                    task_id=task.task_id,\n                    stage_id=stage.stage_id,\n                    stage_type=stage.stage_type,\n                    provider=stage.assigned_provider,\n                )\n\n    async def _run_stage(self, task: TaskRecord, stage: StageRecord) -> None:\n        try:\n            if not stage.assigned_model or not stage.assigned_provider:\n                choice = self.router.select_model(task, stage)\n                self.task_queue.assign_stage_model(\n                    stage.stage_id,\n                    choice.model,\n                    choice.provider,\n                    account_email=choice.account_email,\n                    reason=choice.reason,\n                )\n                stage = self.task_queue.get_stage(stage.stage_id) or stage\n\n            self._ensure_stage_runtime(stage)\n            previous = self.task_queue.list_completed_stages(task.task_id)\n            handoff_path = self.handoff.write_handoff(task, stage, previous)\n            self.task_queue.mark_stage_running(stage.stage_id, str(handoff_path))\n            stage = self.task_queue.get_stage(stage.stage_id) or stage\n            self._emit_stage_event(\"stage_started\", task, stage)\n\n            result = await self.executor.execute(stage, str(handoff_path), task.working_dir)\n            if stage.verify_cmd and task.working_dir:\n                verification = await self.executor.run_verify_cmd(stage.verify_cmd, task.working_dir)\n                if verification.returncode != 0:\n                    raise StageExecutionError(verification.stderr.strip() or verification.stdout.strip() or stage.verify_cmd)\n\n            self.handoff.save_stage_output(task, stage, result)\n            self.task_queue.complete_stage(\n                stage.stage_id,\n                result_summary=result.summary,\n                token_used=result.token_used,\n                duration_sec=result.duration_sec,\n            )\n            self.checkpoints.delete(task.task_id, stage.stage_id)\n            self.task_queue.update_task_status(task.task_id, TaskStatus.EXECUTING)\n            self._emit_stage_event(\n                \"stage_succeeded\",\n                task,\n                stage,\n                duration_sec=result.duration_sec,\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
